title: Add an evaluator/critic Morty reflection loop before closing tickets
source: AutoGen reflection pattern + Anthropic evaluator-optimizer agent pattern (from prior knowledge; live fetch blocked)
description: The evaluator-optimizer / reflection pattern spawns a second agent that adversarially reviews the first agent's output against acceptance criteria before it is accepted. This measurably reduces silently-wrong completions in multi-agent systems. The CTO closes tickets after a worker reports done, but there's no independent verification step. Inserting a lightweight reviewer Morty that checks the diff and the ticket's acceptance criteria — and can bounce the ticket back to 'in progress' — improves correctness without a human in the loop.
proposed_changes: Add a `review_ticket(ticket, handoff)` function that spawns a dedicated reviewer `claude -p` with a critic persona, passing the git diff of files_changed plus the ticket's acceptance_criteria, and returns a structured verdict {approved: bool, issues: list[str]}. In orchestrate.py, gate close_ticket behind this verdict: if not approved, re-delegate with the issues appended (max 2 retries) before escalating. Emit `roro` events for review_started/review_passed/review_failed so the Rick Terminal visualizes the reflection loop.
